request.prompt: We need to hire 5 engineers in the next 2 months. Our current process is just posting on LinkedIn and hoping. Build me a proper hiring pipeline.
I'm a freelance designer. I need a workflow for managing client projects — from initial inquiry to final delivery and getting paid. I keep forgetting to send invoices.

response.message: Mission received. Building your client project management workflow with invoice automation to prevent missed payments.

nodes.label: Client Inquiry Received
nodes.description: Initial contact from potential client via email, form, or call
nodes.content: Capture inquiry details immediately: client name, contact info, project type, budget range, and timeline. Use a CRM tool like HoneyBook or Dubsado to log entries automatically from webhooks. Set up email parsing with tools like Zapier to forward inquiries to your system. Define qualification criteria: minimum budget, project scope alignment, and client responsiveness. Store all data in a centralized database (e.g., Airtable or Notion) for tracking. Include a checklist: 1) Acknowledge receipt within 4 hours, 2) Schedule a discovery call, 3) Send a brief questionnaire to gather specifics. Configure alerts for new inquiries to ensure prompt follow-up and avoid missed opportunities.

nodes.label: Qualify & Schedule Discovery
nodes.description: Assess client fit and book a kickoff meeting
nodes.content: Step 1: Review inquiry against qualification criteria—reject if outside scope with a templated response. Step 2: For qualified leads, send a Calendly link or similar scheduling tool (e.g., Calendly integrated with Google Calendar) to book a 30-minute discovery call. Step 3: Prepare for the call: research client background, draft initial questions about goals, brand guidelines, and deliverables. Use a script template in tools like Loom or Notion. Step 4: During the call, take detailed notes using Otter.ai for transcription, focusing on pain points and success metrics. Step 5: Post-call, send a summary email confirming key points and next steps, and update the CRM status to 'Discovery Complete'. Assign an owner (you) and set a reminder for 24-hour follow-up.

nodes.label: Draft Project Proposal & Contract
nodes.description: Create a detailed proposal outlining scope, timeline, and payment terms
nodes.content: Use a template in tools like PandaDoc or Bonsai to draft the proposal. Include sections: 1) Executive summary of client needs, 2) Detailed scope of work with deliverables (e.g., logo design, 3 revisions), 3) Timeline with milestones (e.g., wireframes in 2 weeks), 4) Pricing breakdown (fixed fee or hourly), 5) Payment schedule (e.g., 50% upfront, 50% on delivery), 6) Terms and conditions (IP transfer, revision limits). Incorporate invoice reminders by setting automatic triggers in the contract—e.g., invoice sent upon milestone completion via integration with QuickBooks or FreshBooks. Add a clause for late fees to incentivize timely payment. Review proposal with a checklist: clarity, legal compliance, and alignment with discussed goals. Save as PDF and share via secure link.

nodes.label: Client Review & Contract Sign-off
nodes.description: Client evaluates and approves the proposal
nodes.content: Send the proposal via email with a tracking link using tools like DocuSign for electronic signatures. Define approval criteria: client agrees to all terms, no scope changes, and signed contract returned within 3 business days. Set up automated reminders for follow-up: day 1, day 3, and day 5 post-send. If revisions are requested, document changes in a change log and update the proposal accordingly—escalate to a call for major changes. Upon approval, notify yourself via Slack or email, and move the project to 'Active' status in the CRM. If rejected, analyze reasons (e.g., budget mismatch) and refine the qualification process, logging insights for future leads. Store signed contract in a cloud folder (Google Drive or Dropbox) with access controls.

nodes.label: Execute Design Work
nodes.description: Carry out design tasks based on approved scope
nodes.content: Break down the project into phases: 1) Research and mood boards using tools like Miro or Figma Jam, 2) Initial drafts (2-3 concepts) in design software (Figma, Adobe Creative Cloud), 3) Internal review with a checklist for brand consistency and usability. Set up a project management tool like Asana or Trello with tasks, deadlines, and dependencies. Use time-tracking apps like Toggl to log hours for billing accuracy. Communicate progress weekly via status updates in a shared Slack channel or email. Implement version control for files using GitHub for designers or Figma versions. Handle feedback iteratively: document all client inputs in a centralized feedback log to avoid scope creep. If blockers arise, escalate immediately with proposed solutions.

nodes.label: Design Review & Feedback Cycle
nodes.description: Client provides feedback on design drafts
nodes.content: Share design drafts via a collaborative platform like Figma Comments or InVision for real-time feedback. Define review criteria: alignment with brand guidelines, functionality, and aesthetic appeal. Set a feedback window (e.g., 48 hours) and send reminders using automated emails from your CRM. Categorize feedback as major (requiring rework) or minor (tweaks). For major changes, assess if they're within scope; if out-of-scope, trigger a change request process with updated pricing. Use a feedback template to capture specific comments and action items. After incorporating feedback, send revised drafts and request final approval. If multiple rounds are needed, limit to 3 as per contract to prevent endless revisions. Log all feedback in the project management tool for audit trails.

nodes.label: Finalize & Deliver Assets
nodes.description: Prepare and hand over final design files
nodes.content: Upon final approval, compile deliverables: source files (e.g., .fig, .ai), exported assets (PNGs, PDFs), style guides, and usage instructions. Use a checklist: 1) Verify all file formats are correct, 2) Optimize images for web and print, 3) Add metadata and copyright info. Package files in a zip folder and upload to a secure delivery platform like WeTransfer or Google Drive with password protection if needed. Send a delivery email with download links, a summary of work completed, and a request for confirmation of receipt. Include a satisfaction survey link (e.g., Typeform) to gather testimonials. Update the CRM to 'Delivered' status and trigger the next step for invoicing automatically via integration with your accounting software.

nodes.label: Generate & Send Invoice with Reminders
nodes.description: Create and dispatch invoice to ensure timely payment
nodes.content: Step 1: Use accounting software like QuickBooks or Wave to generate invoice based on the contract—include details: project name, date, items (e.g., design hours, deliverables), payment terms (net 15 days), and late fee policy. Step 2: Automate invoice creation upon project delivery by setting up a Zapier zap that triggers when CRM status changes to 'Delivered'. Step 3: Send invoice via email with a clear subject line and payment link (e.g., PayPal, Stripe). Step 4: Set up automated reminders: send a thank-you email after 3 days, a gentle reminder at 7 days, and a final notice at 14 days if unpaid. Use tools like Invoice2go for scheduling. Step 5: Track payment status in a dashboard and follow up manually if overdue. Log all communications to avoid disputes.

nodes.label: Payment Received & Project Closure
nodes.description: Confirm payment, archive project, and measure success
nodes.content: Monitor payment notifications from your banking or payment processor (e.g., Stripe webhooks). Upon receipt, send a thank-you email with a receipt and request for a review on platforms like LinkedIn or Clutch. Update financial records: mark invoice as paid in QuickBooks, reconcile accounts, and calculate profit margin. Archive project files in a designated folder (e.g., 'Completed Projects_2024') with a standardized naming convention. Conduct a post-mortem analysis: review timeline adherence, client satisfaction scores, and lessons learned. Document metrics: hours spent vs. budget, revenue generated, and client retention potential. Close the project in the CRM, setting it to 'Archived' status. Optionally, schedule a follow-up in 6 months for potential repeat business or referrals.

checks: ✓ Content depth: avg 759c, no thin nodes
✓ Flow: path exists from first to last node
✓ Architecture: has feedback loops AND parallel branches (10 edges, 9 nodes)

request.prompt: Write a statement of work for a 3-month web application redesign project. Client is a mid-size e-commerce company. Budget is $85,000. Include scope, deliverables, timeline, and payment terms.

response.message:
# Statement of Work: E-Commerce Web Application Redesign

**Project Title:** E-Commerce Platform Redesign & User Experience Enhancement
**Client:** [Client Company Name]
**Vendor:** [Your Agency Name]
**Effective Date:** [Date]
**Project Duration:** 3 Months (Approximately 12 Weeks)
**Total Project Budget:** $85,000.00

---

### **1.0 Project Overview & Objectives**

This Statement of Work (SOW) outlines the agreement between [Client Company Name] ("Client") and [Your Agency Name] ("Vendor") for the comprehensive redesign of the Client's existing e-commerce web application. The primary goal is to modernize the platform's user interface (UI), significantly improve the user experience (UX), enhance site performance, and implement updated front-end technologies to drive increased customer engagement, conversion rates, and average order value (AOV).

Key business objectives include:
*   Increase overall site conversion rate by a target of 15%.
*   Improve mobile conversion rates and reduce bounce rates.
*   Streamline the checkout process to reduce cart abandonment.
*   Create a modern, brand-consistent visual identity that builds trust.
*   Improve site core web vitals and overall performance metrics.
*   Establish a scalable, maintainable front-end architecture.

### **2.0 Scope of Work**

The Vendor will execute the following phases and tasks:

**Phase 1: Discovery & Strategy (Weeks 1-2)**
*   **Stakeholder Workshops:** Conduct collaborative sessions to understand business goals, brand voice, and target audience.
*   **Competitive & Heuristic Analysis:** Review 3-5 competitor sites and perform a UX audit of the current application.
*   **Analytics Review:** Analyze current Google Analytics/Adobe Analytics data to identify pain points and opportunities.
*   **Technical Assessment:** Review current tech stack, identify constraints, and confirm hosting environment compatibility.
*   **Deliverable:** A comprehensive Strategy & Requirements Document.

**Phase 2: User Experience (UX) & Information Architecture (IA) (Weeks 3-5)**
*   **User Personas & Journey Mapping:** Develop detailed personas and map key user flows (browsing, searching, checkout).
*   **Site Mapping:** Redesign the application's information hierarchy and navigation structure.
*   **Wireframing:** Create low-fidelity and high-fidelity interactive wireframes for all key templates, including:
    *   Homepage
    *   Product Listing Pages (PLPs) & Category Pages
    *   Product Detail Pages (PDPs)
    *   Shopping Cart & Checkout Process (including guest checkout)
    *   User Account Dashboard
    *   Content Pages (About, Contact, FAQ)
*   **Usability Testing Plan:** Develop a plan for prototype testing.

**Phase 3: User Interface (UI) Design & Prototyping (Weeks 6-8)**
*   **Visual Design System:** Create a cohesive UI design system including color palette, typography, iconography, button styles, and component library.
*   **High-Fidelity Mockups:** Apply the visual design to all wireframed templates, providing desktop, tablet, and mobile views.
*   **Interactive Prototype:** Develop a clickable prototype (using Figma or similar) of primary user flows for stakeholder review and user testing.
*   **Design Handoff:** Prepare all design assets, specifications, and style guides for development.

**Phase 4: Front-End Development (Weeks 9-11)**
*   **Component Development:** Build responsive, accessible HTML/CSS/JavaScript components based on the approved design system.
*   **Template Integration:** Integrate developed front-end components into the Client's chosen CMS/E-commerce platform (e.g., Shopify Plus, BigCommerce, custom framework). *[Note: Assumes templates are built; back-end data integration is defined by the platform's capabilities.]*
*   **Cross-Browser & Device Testing:** Ensure consistent rendering and functionality across specified browsers and devices.
*   **Performance Optimization:** Optimize images, code, and assets to meet agreed-upon performance benchmarks (e.g., Lighthouse scores).

**Phase 5: Quality Assurance, Launch & Transition (Week 12)**
*   **Functional & User Acceptance Testing (UAT):** Execute a testing plan. Support a 1-week Client UAT period to identify and fix bugs.
*   **Pre-Launch Checklist & Deployment:** Final review, SEO meta-tag population, analytics tag implementation, and staging-to-production deployment.
*   **Post-Launch Support:** Provide 2 weeks of post-launch monitoring and critical bug-fix support.
*   **Knowledge Transfer:** Deliver final documentation and conduct a handoff session with the Client's team.

### **3.0 Deliverables**

1.  Strategy & Requirements Document
2.  User Personas & Journey Maps
3.  Site Map and Updated IA
4.  Complete set of High-Fidelity Wireframes
5.  UI Design System & Component Library
6.  High-Fidelity Mockups for all key page templates (responsive)
7.  Interactive Prototype
8.  Fully developed, responsive front-end templates integrated into the staging environment.
9.  Quality Assurance Test Report
10. Final Project Documentation & Source Files
11. Post-Launch Report (summary of launch and initial observations)

### **4.0 Project Timeline & Milestones**

*   **Kick-off:** Week 1
*   **Milestone 1 – Strategy Sign-off:** End of Week 2
*   **Milestone 2 – UX/IA & Wireframe Sign-off:** End of Week 5
*   **Milestone 3 – UI Design & Prototype Sign-off:** End of Week 8
*   **Milestone 4 – Development Completion & Staging Review:** End of Week 11
*   **Client UAT Period:** Week 12
*   **Launch & Project Closeout:** End of Week 12

*A detailed project schedule with weekly checkpoints will be maintained in Asana/Monday.com and reviewed in weekly status meetings.*

### **5.0 Payment Terms**

The total fixed project fee is **$85,000.00**.

Payments are tied to project milestones and deliverables:
*   **Payment 1 (20% - $17,000):** Due upon SOW execution to initiate project.
*   **Payment 2 (25% - $21,250):** Due upon Client sign-off of the Strategy & Requirements Document (Milestone 1).
*   **Payment 3 (25% - $21,250):** Due upon Client sign-off of UI Designs & Interactive Prototype (Milestone 3).
*   **Payment 4 (30% - $25,500):** Due upon successful launch and project closeout.

All invoices are payable within **15 days** of receipt. Work on subsequent phases is contingent upon timely approval of deliverables and payment of preceding invoices.

### **6.0 Assumptions & Exclusions**

**Assumptions:**
*   Client will assign a single point of contact for approvals and feedback with a 48-hour standard turnaround time for submitted deliverables.
*   Client will provide all necessary brand assets, style guides (if they exist), and logins for current analytics, hosting, and e-commerce platforms in a timely manner.
*   Client will be responsible for providing all final, approved website content (text, images, video) by the start of Development (Week 9).
*   The project scope covers front-end redesign and standard e-commerce template development. Complex new back-end functionality (e.g., custom ERP integration, advanced search algorithms) is out of scope.
*   Ongoing hosting, platform licensing fees, third-party plugin costs, and post-launch content updates are not included.

**Exclusions (Out of Scope):**
*   Copywriting and content creation services.
*   Professional photography or videography.
*   Major back-end development or database architecture.
*   Marketing campaigns or SEO strategy beyond on-page technical implementation.
*   Support beyond the 2-week post-launch period (available under a separate retainer agreement).

request.prompt: What's the best way to set up a data pipeline?

response.message: Voilà! For setting up a data pipeline, mon ami, begin by thoroughly investigating your data sources and business requirements—tools like Apache Airflow or Prefect for orchestration, coupled with data validation frameworks like Great Expectations, will ensure quality and reliability. Monitor key metrics such as data latency, throughput, and error rates using dashboards in Grafana or Datadog, and adopt incremental loading techniques to handle large datasets efficiently. Très intéressant! Always iterate with feedback loops to refine the pipeline, much like solving a complex case with careful clues.

request.prompt: What do we have so far?

response.message: Mon ami, we have a blank canvas—the case file is empty, awaiting our first clue. My little grey cells are ready to build a masterpiece of logic and flow. Shall we begin?

request.prompt: We just got our first EU customer and we have zero GDPR compliance. Build me a workflow to get compliant — data audit, privacy policy, consent management, breach notification process, DPO appointment.
